title: Leucas beddomei
taxonomy: Leucas beddomei is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is classified under the genus Leucas, which is a group of plants commonly known as wild sage or leucas. The species Leucas beddomei is native to the Western Ghats in India. This classification places it within the larger family Lamiaceae, which includes a diverse range of plants such as mint, basil, and rosemary. Further classification would include its kingdom and phylum, but these details are not provided here.

intro: Leucas beddomei, also known as Beddome's leucas, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which have been used to treat a range of health issues including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
